records.ety: [L. fusio, fr. fundere, fusum to pour, melt: cf. F. fusion. See Fuse, v. t., aud cf, Foison.]

defs: The act or operation of melting or rendering fluid by heat; the act of melting together; as, the fusion of metals.
The state of being melted or dissolved by heat; a state of fluidity or flowing in consequence of heat; as, metals in fusion.
The union or blending together of things, as, melted together.
quotes: The universal fusion of races, languages, and customs . . . had produced a corresponding fusion of creeds.

defs: The union, or binding together, of adjacent parts or tissues.
